utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: next patient is nicole miller . date of birth is 09/18/1949 . patient was called for a follow-up with me for chronic congestive heart failure with diastolic dysfunction . bmp's been , uh , 3,000 in march , and is about six- was up to 6,000 in april . she was increasingly dyspneic . we changed her furosemide and torsemide 20 milligrams by mouth daily . uh to note , the patient is not currently on potassium supplement . her lisinopril had- has also been increased up to 10 milligrams daily in march . also did when i saw her last april . she reported being interested in having her right knee replaced this summer at east metro . it was recommended that we work to control her cardiovascular status before surgery .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , miss miller , how are you today ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm doing okay , thank you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i asked you to come in today because we want to keep- we want you to have this knee surgery this summer but we want to keep a close eye on you to make sure a week before your surgery you do n't suddenly go into congestive heart failure and it gets postponed .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , that would not be good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i see you're scheduled on the 24th for surgery .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , that's right .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , good . well it looks like you have lost about 3 , 3 and a half pounds since i saw you last in april . some of that might be water weight , but still , this is positive .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , i noticed that too . i think the oxyglutinine is helping as well . my urgency to use the bathroom is much better .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: well that's great .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , i , i'm pleased about it too .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: you ever get leg or finger cramps or anything like that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , i had leg cramps the other day , but i thought it might , was maybe just because i was cold as i had my ceiling fan on and fell asleep . i had cramps when i woke up in both legs right here . um i drank pickle juice and it went right away .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: well do n't , do n't get crazy with the pickle juice because all of the salt in it .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: haha , i know , i only drink about 4 ounces or so .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay good .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um it went away so i did n't drink anymore . i find it works a lot better than trying to put some cream on my leg .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: sure just , just keep it in moderation .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and then are you still on an iron supplement ? and are you using the bathroom okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh yes , everything is good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: good . how is your heart burn doing ? any problems with that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , it did get bad for a while so i tried to take some prilosec and then stopped that other one .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um i did that for like , gosh , i think it was two weeks back in january and have n't had any problems since .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: great .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um and after i stopped taking that um i went back to the stomach one , so i'm doing good now .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and you're still due for a colonoscopy , correct ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh yeah , that's right .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right , let's review your blood work real quick . i checked your hemoglobin level because you have had some anemia in the past but that is still doing great .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: good , that's a relief to hear .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: your potassium is 3.9 so it's holding steady on the torsemide . your creatinine was .7 not .8 so you're doing well with kidney numbers . your bun may be a tiny bit elevated at 23 which is the number we look for for dehydration sometimes the kidneys , but it's not terrible . um so when i look at your numbers as a whole i think you're tolerating the torsemide okay at the current dose . i also sent out to look at the heart failure number- i sent to look at your heart failure number . there is a test called a bmp that i was monitoring and in march it was up to 3,000 and then went up to 6,000 in april before i made the change . i'm still waiting for those results .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all in all i think you're doing good on paper though .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: what about , um what's it called , a1c ? does that show up ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um i do n't think i ordered it but i could . your last a1c was 5.5 in march .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: all righty .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so your blood sugar is a little bit high , it was 169 today but that kind of depends on what you ate and you were n't fasting for the blood check so i might have to repeat that test for pre-op , but i do n't think we need to do it today .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: all righty that sounds good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i checked your magnesium level because sometimes you uh urinate out magnesium with the water pills but it was normal at 1.7 and your blood pressure is also looking good .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay great . that all sounds awesome .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right let's take a quick listen .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: use my general physical exam template .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and take a couple of deep breaths for me .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: your lungs sound pretty good to me so keep doing what you're doing . um uh , like i said , i think you're doing good overall but let's just talk about a few things .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: all righty .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so we often like to keep people with heart problems on magnesium and get their levels up to around the 2-ish range . yours is a little bit less than 2 and we want that 2-ish range because it can help stabilize the heart muscle . so i might recommend putting you on magnesium supplement . it's supposed to be twice a day so that's kind of annoying , but i know you're on other medicines twice a day too , so i think you'll do fine .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , that'll be okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: great . now before surgery we'll have to get you off your clopidogrel for a week beforehand .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , okay , i have everything written down on my phone , and i have a letter taped to the side of my bed to remind me .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: perfect ! we will give you a reminder as well . we will also need to complete a pre-op check within two weeks of your surgery during the first or second week of june .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , i'll put that down .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: you might also have to repeat an ekg before surgery which we could do today . i know i'm sure it feels like you're doing , you're always doing ekgs . um we do n't need to any x-rays of your chest because you had one recently , and we do n't need any more blood work because we did that today .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , i do a lot of ekgs . i'm basically a regular . but i'm happy to do one today , no problem .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: lastly , once we get your knee surgery , um we , we should think about getting you a colonoscopy . we can do it here locally because you have medicare . do you have private insurance also ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , i have both .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay so yes , you can get it , your colonoscopy , wherever you'd like .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , well my husband's insurance may be running out . might we be able to get the procedure done sooner ? maybe in the next 30 days ? is that okay ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um i can put it in right now for , uh , for county for the next 30 days , and they might be able to get you in within the next few weeks . it should not take , it should not make you ineligible for the surgery . in other words completing a colonoscopy would not delay your surgery .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so let me see . i've been doing one of two things at every one , and everyone is great so it depends more on timing availability of their or for the colonoscopy . we can send you to dr. martin for the surgery who is at county surgical services down here or the other option is valley medical , and they do it at springfield .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , that sounds good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i think either direction they're good technicians of the colon .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , yeah whatever you can get me in , that works great .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so i'll call around . now if you get that done and they tell you 10 years then you'll be good to go .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: great , thank you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: you're welcome . have a great day . let us know if you need anything else , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sounds good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right , assessment and plan .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: chronic chf . mixed presentation . had a exacerbation of cf , chf earlier in the spring . we switched her from a furosemide to torsemide and symptomatically she is doing a lot better . she's about 3 , 3 and a half pounds down in weight . breathing is non-labored . going to repeat ekg today but otherwise continue with her current regimen . labs checked and creatinine is appropriate .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: uh number 2 , pre-op examination . she is , she's having a right knee replacement end of june . also , she would like to have a colonoscopy performed which we'll try to have done at uh bartley regional , rightley regional hospital in the next month , uh , prior to a change in her insurance . this is just a screening colonoscopy that she is overdue for . no family history of colon cancer .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: uh the next one is diabetes . a1c is 5.1 on the last check so no need for further a1c today . she may need another one prior to her surgery next month though . thanks .

detail_list.summary: Nicole Miller is a 71-year-old female presenting for follow-up of chronic congestive heart failure with diastolic dysfunction.
The patient's BNP was 3000 in March and 6000 in April.
The patient was switched to torsemide 20 mg daily.
The patient is not currently taking a potassium supplement.
Lisinopril was increased to 10 mg daily in March.
The patient is interested in right knee replacement surgery at East Metropolitan Hospital.
Preoperative clearance for knee surgery.
The patient is scheduled for surgery on 06/24/2021.
The patient has lost approximately 3 to 3.5 pounds since April.
The doctor asks if the patient experiences leg or finger cramps.
The patient reports bilateral leg cramps treated with pickle juice.
The doctor asks if the patient is taking iron supplements and if she has any concerns with defecation.
The patient confirms she is taking iron and has no concerns with defecation.
The doctor asks about the patient's heartburn status.
The patient reports heartburn resolved after taking Prilosec for two weeks in January.
Colonoscopy
The doctor reviews blood work, noting hemoglobin is within normal limits.
The patient's potassium is 3.9, creatinine is 0.7, and BUN is 23.
The patient asks about her A1c level.
The patient's last A1c was 5.5 in March.
The patient's magnesium level is 1.7 and blood pressure is normal.
The doctor performs a physical exam and notes the patient's lungs sound clear.
The doctor recommends starting a magnesium supplement twice daily.
The patient must discontinue clopidogrel one week prior to surgery.
The patient needs a pre-op checkup during the first or second week of June.
The patient will have an EKG performed today.
The doctor asks if the patient has private insurance in addition to Medicare.
The patient asks if the colonoscopy can be performed within the next 30 days.
The doctor will schedule the colonoscopy at County Hospital within the next 30 days.
